title: Class imbalance and race-group sample weighting
summary: Test whether moderate positive-class weighting or Race-Year balancing improves ranking without distorting high-prevalence contexts.
rationale: The target is imbalanced and rows are unevenly distributed across races and seasons. Tree models may overweight common race contexts or under-rank positive examples in sparse contexts.
implementation_hint: Compare three training modes for LightGBM and CatBoost: no weights, mild positive-class weight, and inverse Race-Year frequency times mild positive-class weight. Keep the model and features fixed to isolate the weighting effect.
expected_effect: Small AUC or robustness gain with low-medium confidence.
risk: AUC does not require calibrated class balance, and aggressive weighting can hurt if test sampling mirrors training row frequencies. Use mild weights only.
